# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php

// Plugin Name: Custom User Authentication
// Description: A custom WordPress plugin for user registration and authentication with password hashing.

class Custom_User_Authentication {
    public function __construct() {
        add_action('init', array($this, 'handle_user_registration'));
        add_action('wp_login', array($this, 'handle_user_login'), 10, 2);
    }

    public function handle_user_registration() {
        if (isset($_POST['register'])) {
            $username = $_POST['username'];
            $email = $_POST['email'];
            $password = $_POST['password'];

            // Validate input
            if (empty($username) || empty($email) || empty($password)) {
                echo 'All fields are required.';
                return;
            }

            // Hash password
            $hashed_password = password_hash($password, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);

            // Create user
            $user_id = wp_insert_user(array(
                'user_login' => $username,
                'user_email' => $email,
                'user_pass' => $hashed_password
            ));

            if (!is_wp_error($user_id)) {
                echo 'User registered successfully.';
            } else {
                echo 'Registration failed.';
            }
        }
    }

    public function handle_user_login($user_login, $user) {
        // Handle user login logic here if needed
    }
}

new Custom_User_Authentication();

// Shortcode for registration form
function custom_user_registration_form() {
    ob_start();
?>
    <form method="post">
        Username: <input type="text" name="username"><br>
        Email: <input type="email" name="email"><br>
        Password: <input type="password" name="password"><br>
        <input type="submit" name="register" value="Register">
    </form>
<?php
    return ob_get_clean();
}
add_shortcode('user_registration_form', 'custom_user_registration_form');
?>
